data.narrative:
Near Space Labs, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Rema Matevosyan, has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, with 3 direct same-stage peers identified in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two valuation methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $40M total raised proxy, implying a $76M valuation. Peer-median analysis examined 3 Series B space companies—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—yielding a median of $400M. Public-comparable anchoring could not be applied due to the absence of target revenue data, a limitation typical of private companies. The public sector median P/S multiple across the 5 tickers stands at 59.8x, provided for context but inapplicable here.

The triangulated range spans $65M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $238M across the two applicable methods. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($274M–$1.3B), which reflects heterogeneous business models and capital deployment strategies within space infrastructure. The stage-progression result sits substantially below peer medians, suggesting either that Near Space Labs operates at lower maturity or revenue scale than benchmarks, or that the $40M raised figure underweights post-money valuation history. Resolution of revenue traction would enable public-comp anchoring and tighten the range materially.
